rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. BRYAN KELTNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED/CONTEMPT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 19, 2024 line 1. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED/CONTEMPT is OFF CALENDAR. Counsel filed a Declaration on December 13, 2024 providing a satisfactory explanation for failure to comply with the Court's order re: personal appearance regarding a matter that could potentially involve an in-camera review. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LILIAN PADUA ET AL VS. VI THANH LE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 19, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT VIRGIL MANDAC TRUSTEE OF THE LUCAS FAMILY TRUST, VIRGIL MANDAC, PATRICIA PERALTA, PATRICIA PERALTA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT L. BASSINETTE VS. STEPHEN WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Cross-Complaint From Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 19, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF ROBERT BASSINETTE AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Cross-Complaint From Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Bifurcation of the Cross-Complaint filed by the defendants will not achieve the Plaintiff's desired goal of setting their Complaint for trial, as on November 26, 2024 another Cross-Complaint by a third party was filed and is not yet at issue. Plaintiff's motion filed before the November 26, 2024 cross-complaint understandably did not address it or it's effect on setting the complaint for trial. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
